title: Refactor SRP-007 (SRP): The ReflectiveTypeAdapterFactory orchestrates a complex process of class intr...

detected_issue.description: The ReflectiveTypeAdapterFactory orchestrates a complex process of class introspection, applying various configuration rules (exclusion, naming, access), and creating specialized adapters for different class structures.
detected_issue.reasoning: This class exhibits the God Class smell. It is responsible for a multitude of concerns related to reflective type adaptation: delegating field exclusion to `excluder`, determining JSON field names based on `fieldNamingPolicy` and `@SerializedName` (`getFieldNames`), applying reflection access filters, handling different class types (regular classes vs. Java Records), creating object constructors via `constructorConstructor`, managing `@JsonAdapter` annotations, building `BoundField` representations, and traversing the class hierarchy (`getBoundFields`). This broad and intricate set of responsibilities means it has many independent reasons to change, such as updates to field naming conventions, new record features, or changes in reflection access rules, violating SRP.

fix_logic.summary: Extract field naming logic into a dedicated helper class
fix_logic.rationale: The ReflectiveTypeAdapterFactory was handling field naming policy logic directly, which is a distinct responsibility. Moving this to a helper class reduces the size and complexity of the factory, adhering better to SRP.
